pages.title: Frameworks | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations to create and manage green bond frameworks, including framework registration, year-specific details, and category/indicator assignments. Enables building comprehensive frameworks that define ESG benefit categories, impact indicators, and allocated funding amounts for green investment reporting.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all private or public green frameworks registered for a specific reporting year

useCases: Create green bond frameworks with defined use of proceeds categories
Register renewable energy and energy efficiency project frameworks
Define ESG impact indicators for sustainable investment reporting
Track framework funding allocation and value outstanding
Build public frameworks visible across different BWS accounts

implementationSteps.title: Create Framework Base
implementationSteps.description: Use addFramework to register framework identifier, name, currency code, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Add Year-Specific Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToFramework to set value outstanding, allocated amount, and reporting year parameters

implementationSteps.title: Assign Categories and Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToFramework to define sub-categories, funding volumes, and ESG benefit indicators with values

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Framework Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getFrameworks and getFrameworkDetails to access complete framework information for reporting

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getFrameworks parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "framework_id" : 3 , "framework_identifier" : "greenbond" , "framework_name" : "Good Bank Green Bond Framework" , "framework_value_outstanding" : 22224856800 , "framework_allocated_amount" : 22224856800 , "framework_active_percentage" : 1.0 , "framework_currency_code" : "SEK" , "value_outstanding_framework" : "yes" , "framework_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such framework is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy framework_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl getFrameworkDetails Returns details about a green framework for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operations to retrieve green framework details. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getFrameworkDetails parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (sexample) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl addFramework Creates and updates the information about a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add green frameworks to the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addFramework parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework framework_name string yes Framework name framework_currency_code string yes Currency code of the framework, as per getCurrencies framework_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addYearSpecificsToFramework Adds reporting year specific details to a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the green framework with details that are year specific. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addYearSpecificsToFramework parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework value_outstanding number yes Amount of funding raised to green projects for the framework as a whole, for the reporting year allocated_amount number yes Amount of funding allocated to projects for the reporting year of the framework, for the corresponding impacts registered value_outstanding_framework string yes Choose "yes" when use of proceeds relate to value outstanding, choose "no" when related to allocated amount reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl addCategoryIndicatorToFramework Adds and updates category and indicator values to a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the different impact categories, indicators, and units, with specified values. pages.title: Impacts | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents impact calculation and retrieval operations for green asset investments. Provides APIs to calculate ESG benefits based on investment positions, retrieve impact data for investors and portfolios, and generate impact reports showing environmental benefits like CO2 emission reductions and renewable energy production.

codeExamples.description: Calculates and stores the ESG impact benefits for a specific investment position in a green asset

useCases: Calculate CO2 emission reduction benefits from renewable energy investments
Generate investor impact reports showing environmental contributions
Track portfolio-level ESG benefits across multiple green assets
Calculate impact benefits without storing investor data
Create impact certificates for carbon offset programs

implementationSteps.title: Register Investment Position
implementationSteps.description: Create a position record with investment amount, currency, and term using the Positions API

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Impact Benefits
implementationSteps.description: Use calculatePositionImpacts to compute ESG benefits based on the investment size and framework allocation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Impact Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositionImpacts, getInvestorImpacts, or getPortfolioImpacts to access calculated environmental benefits

Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed. getPositionImpacts Returns the impacts of an investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestorImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor asset_identifier string no For a specified asset of an investor reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getPortfolioImpacts Returns the impacts of an investor's portfolio of investments in green assets POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identfier string yes String identifier for a portfolio asset_identifier string no For a specified asset in a portfolio reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl calculatePositionImpacts Calculates and stores the impact of an investor's investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a position position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/ 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl calculatePositionImpactsOnly Calculates and returns the impact of an investment in a green asset, without having to specify the investor and without storing the result POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpactsOnly parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description asset_identifier string yes String identifier for an asset position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a portfolio
For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits.
calculatePositionImpacts
check Operation parameters
Integer identifier for a position
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.
calculatePositionImpactsOnly
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Manages investor data including registration, encryption of personally identifiable information (PII), and investor identifier generation. Supports creating encrypted investor records, generating random identifiers for privacy, and retrieving investor information with decryption keys for secure ESG investment tracking.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an investor record with encrypted personally identifiable information
Generates a secure encryption key for protecting personally identifiable investor information

useCases: Register green bond investors with encrypted personal data
Generate random investor identifiers for privacy protection
Manage investor portfolios with secure PII encryption
Create investor correspondence records with email addresses
Track multi-currency investor preferences

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Use generateEncryptionKey to create a secure key for encrypting investor PII data

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Identifier
implementationSteps.description: Call generateInvestorIdentifier for random IDs or use custom identifiers based on your requirements

implementationSteps.title: Register Investor
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestor with investor details and encryption key to securely store investor information

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Investor Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getInvestors with decryption key to access investor information when needed

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Manages issuers of green assets including registration, retrieval, and removal operations. Enables tracking of organizations that issue green bonds and sustainable financial instruments, with support for public and private issuer visibility settings across different BWS accounts.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an issuer of green assets with specified currency and visibility settings

useCases: Register banks and financial institutions issuing green bonds
Track corporate issuers of sustainable financial instruments
Manage public issuer databases accessible across BWS accounts
Associate green assets with their issuing organizations
Maintain private issuer lists for proprietary portfolios

implementationSteps.title: Register Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer to create issuer records with identifier, name, currency code, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuer List
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers to access registered issuers, filtering by public or private visibility

implementationSteps.title: Associate with Assets
implementationSteps.description: Link issuers to green assets when creating asset records in the Assets API

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIssuers parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error messagee Copy [ { "issuer_id" : 1 , "issuer_identifier" : "35f0c1a1c08" , "issuer_name" : "X Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "EUR" , "issuer_visibility" : "public" } , { "issuer_id" : 6 , "issuer_identifier" : "good-bank-issuer" , "issuer_name" : "Good Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "SEK" , "issuer_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such issuer is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer Example code curl Previous Projects Next Assets Last updated 9 days ago Was this helpful? getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. Asset(s) exist." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "removeIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer" }

pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides portfolio management operations for organizing green asset investments. Enables creating investor portfolios, adding positions to portfolios, retrieving portfolio holdings, and consolidating impact data across multiple investments for comprehensive ESG reporting at the portfolio level.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new portfolio for an investor to organize their green asset investments
Adds a specific investment position to an investor's portfolio for consolidated reporting

useCases: Organize investor holdings into thematic green portfolios
Track multiple green bond investments in a single portfolio
Generate consolidated ESG impact reports at portfolio level
Manage diversified sustainable investment strategies
Compare impact performance across different investor portfolios

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio to establish a portfolio container for an investor with a unique identifier

implementationSteps.title: Add Investment Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio to associate specific green asset investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios and getPortfolioPositions to access portfolio holdings and investment details

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Portfolio Impacts
implementationSteps.description: Utilize the Impacts API getPortfolioImpacts operation to retrieve consolidated ESG benefits

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolios parameters * string check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "portfolio_id" : 1 , "portfolio_identifier" : "portfolio_john" , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "assets" : [ { "asset_identifier" : "X123456" , "asset_name" : "Green Deposits" } , { "asset_identifier" : "XS24427682" , "asset_name" : "Asset X, 5-year" } , { "asset_identifier" : "XS25798443" , "asset_name" : "Asset Y, 4-year" } ] } , { "portfolio_id" : 3 , "portfolio_identifier" : "portfolio_mary" , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "assets" : [ { "asset_identifier" : "XS25798443" , "asset_name" : "Asset Y, 4-year" } ] } ] Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor asset_identifier string no String identifier for an asset to include. Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioPositions parameters * JSON check Operation Parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation Parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create a portfolio for an investor, next to be associated with specific investments. This operation is also used to alter already existing portfolio information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addPortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation Parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation Parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio portfolio_identifier_changed string no Used to change the portfolio identifier Example code curl addPositionToPortfolio Adds an investment in a green asset to a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to associate particular investments to a portfolio. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addPositionToPortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identfier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for an investment Example code curl removePositionFromPortfolio Removes an investment in a green asset from a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove positions from a portfolio, prior to removal of the investment as such. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removePositionFromPortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identfier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for an investment Example code curl removePortfolio Removes a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove a portfolio, prior to removing the investor. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removePortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl Previous Positions Next Impacts Last updated 9 days ago Was this helpful? getPortfolios Returns all portfolios of all investors getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor addPositionToPortfolio Adds an investment in a green asset to a portfolio of an investor removePositionFromPortfolio Removes an investment in a green asset from a portfolio of an investor removePortfolio Removes a portfolio of an investor Was this helpful? pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Manages investment positions in green assets, including creating, retrieving, and removing investor holdings. Enables tracking investment amounts, currencies, terms, and start dates for calculating ESG impacts and generating investor reports for green bonds and sustainable financial instruments.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new investment position for an investor in a specific green asset with amount and term details

useCases: Register investor purchases of green bonds
Track short-term and long-term sustainable investments
Record investment dates for reporting period calculations
Associate investments with specific investors and assets
Enable automatic ESG impact calculation upon position creation

implementationSteps.title: Register Investment
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor ID, asset ID, investment amount, currency, and optional term/start date

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Impacts
implementationSteps.description: If reporting_year is provided, impacts are automatically calculated; otherwise use calculatePositionImpacts separately

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions to access all investments for a specific investor, optionally filtering by asset or issuer

implementationSteps.title: Remove When Needed
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition to delete investment records and associated impact data when positions are closed

String identifier for a specified issuer to include. Default is all.
For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to
For decrypting personally identifiable information

String identifier for an investor

position_currency_code

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Manages green projects and their environmental impact characteristics, including project registration, impact indicators, and framework consolidation. Enables building project-level ESG data that can be aggregated into green bond frameworks, supporting detailed tracking of renewable energy, energy efficiency, and circular economy initiatives.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new green project with identifier, name, currency, and associated framework

useCases: Register renewable energy projects like wind farms and solar installations
Track energy efficiency improvement projects
Document circular economy and waste reduction initiatives
Build green bond frameworks from multiple project impacts
Create public project databases for transparency

implementationSteps.title: Create Project Base
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject to register project with identifier, name, currency, and framework association

implementationSteps.title: Add Year-Specific Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject to set allocated funding amount for the reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Define Impact Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject to assign categories, volumes, indicators, and ESG benefit values

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate into Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use buildFrameworkFromProjects to aggregate multiple project impacts into a single framework

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Defines the ESG taxonomy structure including categories, sub-categories, indicators, units, and sustainability frameworks. Provides operations to retrieve UN Sustainable Development Goals, EU Environmental Objectives, and build custom taxonomies with impact measurement standards for green finance reporting.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals for taxonomy reference

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies aligned with UN SDGs
Map green projects to EU Environmental Objectives
Define impact measurement categories for renewable energy
Create standardized units for emission reduction reporting
Associate sustainability indicators with investment frameworks

implementationSteps.title: Explore Standard Frameworks
implementationSteps.description: Use getSDGs and getEUObjectives to review available sustainability goals and objectives

implementationSteps.title: Build Category Structure
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory and addSubCategory to create taxonomy hierarchy for your reporting needs

implementationSteps.title: Define Indicators and Units
implementationSteps.description: Add impact indicators and measurement units to quantify ESG benefits

implementationSteps.title: Associate Goals
implementationSteps.description: Use addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory to link sub-categories with UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives

pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing multilingual translations in ESG Credits taxonomy. Covers operations to retrieve available languages and add translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units across different reporting years.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all registered languages available for taxonomy translations
Adds or changes a Swedish translation for a category in the 2023 taxonomy
Adds a language translation to an indicator for a specific reporting year

useCases: Create multilingual ESG reporting frameworks for international investors
Translate environmental impact indicators into multiple European languages
Localize sustainability taxonomy for regional compliance requirements
Add Swedish translations for water management indicators
Support multi-language ESG reporting dashboards

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to list all supported language codes (en, sv, dk, nb, fi, de, pl, fr, nl, es, pt, it) for translation registration

implementationSteps.title: Add Taxonomy Translations
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation, addIndicatorTranslation operations with language_code, reporting_year, and translation parameters

implementationSteps.title: Translate Units
implementationSteps.description: Register unit translations using addUnitTranslation operation, even if units are identical between languages, as required by the model

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing end users, groups, and investor access control in ESG Credits. Provides operations to create/update/remove users, assign users to groups (bankers, investors, regulators), and manage user access to specific investor portfolios.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user or updates existing user information with encrypted data
Assigns a user to the bankers group for access control
Grants a user access to view specific investor portfolio information

useCases: Create user accounts for bank advisors accessing ESG investment data
Assign regulatory users to compliance groups for audit access
Control investor access to specific green asset portfolios
Manage role-based permissions for ESG reporting platforms
Grant venture capitalists access to project sponsor portfolios

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, role (user or admin), and optional encryption_key to create or update user accounts

implementationSteps.title: Assign Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call addGroupToUser with available groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators) to control functionality access

implementationSteps.title: Configure Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser operation to connect users with specific investors they can access, enabling portfolio-level permission control

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the ESG Credits solution for financial institutions adding ESG impact reporting to digital channels. Designed for banks, investment advisors, institutional investors, and venture capitalists managing green asset portfolios with automated impact calculations based on ICMA frameworks or custom taxonomies.
useCases: Banks creating digital ESG reports for customers investing in green assets
Institutional investors viewing consolidated impact reports for green portfolios
Venture capitalists providing sponsors with project-level impact reports
Asset issuers publishing impact data for promotional and calculation purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured green project characteristics
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Calendar objects representing scheduled games, matches, or tournaments in FAN Game Cube. Calendars are time-bound events associated with Fields, enabling season-based or single-event fan campaigns like UEFA Champions League Finals or full LaLiga seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for LaLiga 2025-26 season and returns calendarId
Lists all calendars or retrieves a specific calendar by ID
Deletes a calendar by its calendarId

useCases: Schedule UEFA Champions League Final as single-event calendar
Create LaLiga full season calendar with multiple matches
Manage tournament timelines for fan engagement campaigns
Link game schedules to tokenized field zones
Organize time-bound NFT-based sports competitions

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with name parameter (e.g., 'LaLiga 2025-26') to create a scheduled event and receive calendarId for linking

implementationSteps.title: List Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars operation optionally with calendarId to retrieve all calendars or a specific calendar's details

implementationSteps.title: Delete Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Remove a calendar using delete_calendar operation with the calendarId parameter

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Calendar Represents a scheduled game, match, or tournament instance —essentially, a time-bound event associated with a specific Field. The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "new_calendar" , "parameters" : { "name" : "2025-26" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js list_calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of calendars you created or a single calendar if the CalendarId parameter is included in the call. delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js delete_calendar Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Cubes, which are tokenized zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by fans, tracking ownership, enabling rewards, and reflecting live event impacts during matches. Cubes are auto-generated when creating fields.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a field with pagination support, returning cube data including coordinates and status
Updates pricing for multiple cubes to $5 USD each, identified by their i_j indexes

useCases: Sell NFT field zones to fans for ownership and rewards
Update cube pricing based on field position or demand
Track fan ownership of virtual stadium sections
Enable live event rewards for cube owners during matches
Create premium pricing for goal zone cubes

implementationSteps.title: List Field Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes operation with fieldId and limit parameters to retrieve cubes with their coordinates (x, y, i, j), status, and cubeId. Use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Prices
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList array (using i_j format like '40_0'), and priceInCents to set USD pricing for specific cubes

implementationSteps.title: Handle Pagination
implementationSteps.description: If response includes lastEvaluatedKey, include it in subsequent list_cubes calls to retrieve the next chunk of cubes from fields with hundreds of zones

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for Event Types, which define in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or passes in FAN Game Cube. Events are registered with spatial coordinates and timestamps, affecting cube owners whose zones match the action location with configurable point values.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new pass event type worth 100 points and returns eventTypeId
Updates the point value of an existing event type to 1000 points
Lists all event types configured for a specific field with their point values

useCases: Define goal events worth high points for cube owners
Create pass events with lower point values for frequent actions
Configure assist event types for rewarding playmaker zones
Set up foul events with negative points in penalty areas
Manage event scoring systems for different sports types

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type operation with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass', 'goal'), and eventTypePoints to define in-game occurrences and their point values, receiving eventTypeId

implementationSteps.title: List Available Events
implementationSteps.description: Call list_event-types with fieldId to retrieve all configured event types, their IDs, names, and assigned points

implementationSteps.title: Update or Delete Events
implementationSteps.description: Modify point values using update_event-type or remove events with delete_event-type, both requiring eventTypeId and fieldId parameters

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for Field objects, representing virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive zones called Cubes. Fields are the foundational canvas for game interactions, typically linked to real-world sports venues, with configurable dimensions and automatic cube generation.

codeExamples.description: Creates Camp Nou football field with 120m x 80m dimensions and 3m x 3m cubes, returns fieldId
Lists all fields or retrieves a specific field by fieldId with details
Deletes a field and all associated cubes automatically

useCases: Create virtual Real Madrid Stadium for NFT-based fan games
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into interactive zones
Build Camp Nou digital field with 3x3 meter cube sections
Configure cricket pitch with custom dimensions for season play
Generate chess board fields for tournament gameplay

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field operation with name, sport, fieldLength/fieldWidth (in centimeters), and cubeLength parameters to create a virtual field. All cubes are automatically generated upon creation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Fields
implementationSteps.description: Call list_fields optionally with fieldId to get all created fields or specific field details including dimensions, sport type, and status

implementationSteps.title: Delete Field
implementationSteps.description: Remove field using delete_field operation with fieldId. This automatically deletes all associated cubes created for that field

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for Live Events, the core mechanism for recording in-match occurrences at specific coordinates on a Field during scheduled Matches. Events capture game data like goals, moves, and fouls, mapping them to Cubes for scoring and rewards through manual, AI-driven, or application interfaces.

codeExamples.description: Records a live event at coordinates (45, 40) during a match, triggering rewards for affected cube owners

useCases: Record goal events in real-time for instant fan rewards
Capture chess moves automatically via AI model integration
Manually log match events through organizer dashboard
Track foul locations to affect penalty area cube owners
Process live basketball plays for Madison Square Garden games

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, and event details including x/y coordinates and eventTypeId for the occurrence

implementationSteps.title: Record Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with all required parameters to record the in-match event at specific coordinates, which maps to corresponding Cubes

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically identifies affected cube owners based on coordinates and applies scoring/rewards according to the event type's configured points

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in the FAN Game Cube system. A Match represents a time-bound gameplay session tied to a Field where events are tracked, points awarded, and fan interactions occur. Covers operations to create, update, list, and delete matches with team information, images, and pricing.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match in the NFT Game Cube system with team names, flags, start time, and match description
Lists matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by matchId to retrieve a single match's details
Response showing match details including IPFS-hosted images and match status

useCases: Schedule football matches like PSG vs Manchester City for NFT game cubes
Track chess tournament rounds like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Manage sports event sessions with team information and pricing
Create time-bound gameplay sessions for fan engagement
Update match status from scheduled to playing to finished

implementationSteps.title: Create New Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, description, team names and flags (base64 encoded images), and optional pricing in USD cents

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match operation with matchId and calendarId to modify any match parameters like description, status (scheduled/playing/finished/canceled), or team information

implementationSteps.title: List and Monitor Matches
implementationSteps.description: Retrieve match information using list_matches with calendarId to view all matches or specific matchId for individual match details including IPFS URLs for images

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. Previous Plays (Field-Calendar) Next Prizes Last updated 8 days ago Was this helpful? new_match Request Body new_match API Call Example update_match Request Body update_match API Call Example list_matches Request Body list_matches API Call Example delete_match Request Body delete_match API Call Example Was this helpful? pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Field-Calendar relationships in NFT Game Cube. This models the temporal lifecycle where a Field represents static spatial configuration (like a 12×8 football pitch) and Calendar entries represent individual matches or tournaments on that Field. Enables linking seasons and events to playing fields.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field-calendar relationship to link a sports field with a tournament or season calendar
Response showing created field-calendar relationship with IPFS-hosted image URL

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 tournament to stadium field
Associate New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season with basketball court
Manage multiple seasons on the same sports field
Create temporal relationships between static field layouts and dynamic match schedules
Organize tournament calendars for different sports venues

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar operation with fieldId and calendarId to link a sports field's spatial configuration with a tournament or season calendar, including description and base64 encoded image

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Call list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendars/seasons associated with a specific field, showing names and IPFS-hosted images

implementationSteps.title: Delete Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Remove field-calendar link using delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId to end a season or tournament association

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in FAN Game Cube. Prizes represent reward assets or benefits that users receive based on CUBE performance in specific matches or seasons. Can include digital NFTs, token bonuses, physical goods, or experiences to drive fan engagement and incentivize cube ownership.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match prize with position (1st, 2nd, or 3rd), name, description, and image snapshot
Response showing prize details including position ranking and IPFS-hosted snapshot image

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top cube performers
Distribute VIP match tickets as prizes for winners
Offer Zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as rewards
Grant bonus BWS tokens based on cube performance
Create multi-tier prize structures for 1st, 2nd, and 3rd place winners

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize operation with matchId, calendarId, position (1, 2, or 3), prize name, description, and snapshot image (base64 encoded) to define rewards for cube performance

implementationSteps.title: List Match Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Call list_match-prizes with matchId to retrieve all prizes configured for a specific match, including IPFS URLs for prize images

implementationSteps.title: Update or Delete Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Modify prize details with update_match-prize using prizeId, or remove prizes with delete_match-prize to manage reward configurations
