pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of BWS FAN Game Cube platform that revolutionizes fan engagement through NFT-based digital assets linked to sports field sections. Covers multi-sport applications, cube allocation strategies, NFT technology, pricing models, one-click purchase widget, and revenue potential for sports clubs and organizations across football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, and more.
useCases: Tokenize football stadium sections into 500-2000 cubes for match-based fan engagement
Enable chess tournament participation by tokenizing 64 chessboard squares
Create revenue streams for sports clubs through NFT cube sales and secondary market royalties
Allow fans to trade cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces
Launch weekend chess tournaments or full NBA seasons with digital cube ownership
Generate $250,000 revenue by selling 500 cubes at $500 average price for VIP events

implementationSteps.title: Define Sport and Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine how to divide your playing area into cubes based on sport characteristics (football: 500-2000 cubes, chess: 64 squares, basketball: key zones). Identify premium areas like penalty boxes, center court, or try zones for higher pricing

implementationSteps.title: Set Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Configure dynamic pricing with prime field areas at premium prices and secondary zones at lower entry points ($10+). Calculate expected revenue as: Number of cubes × Average Price = Total income

implementationSteps.title: Integrate One-Click Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed the JavaScript widget on your club website for frictionless credit card transactions. No special accounts needed - fans can purchase cubes as easily as buying game tickets

implementationSteps.title: Launch and Enable Real-Time Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Publish the widget and allow fans to manage cube collections, track upcoming matches, join live leaderboards, and compete for prizes through the dedicated mobile app

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 4 days ago Was this helpful? Monitor community impact using official X API data

pages.title: X Bot Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete command reference for X Bot organized by category including account management, keyword tracking, cashtag monitoring, mention tracking, exclusions, ignore lists, named filters, scheduling, and report generation. Covers filtering setup, performance scoring customization, and automated reporting with detailed parameter descriptions and usage examples for each command.

codeExamples.description: Add X (Twitter) accounts to track their posts and engagement metrics, optionally assigning to a named filter
Add keywords or hashtags to track community discussions, using quotes for multi-word phrases
Add cryptocurrency cashtags like $BTC or $ETH to monitor token discussions

useCases: Track multiple X accounts for KOL team performance monitoring
Monitor project-specific hashtags and cashtags for community engagement
Create named filters for different clients or campaigns
Schedule automated daily reports at specific UTC times
Exclude spam keywords to improve report quality
Generate leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement
Customize point weights for different engagement types
Hide test accounts from leaderboards using ignore lists

implementationSteps.title: Configure Tracking Filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts, /add_keywords, /add_cashtags, and /add_mentions commands to define what X content to track. Optionally use filter=name parameter to create multiple independent tracking configurations

implementationSteps.title: Set Exclusions and Ignore Lists
implementationSteps.description: Add keywords to exclude with /add_exclusions to filter out spam, and use /add_ignore_list to hide specific accounts from leaderboards while still tracking their data

implementationSteps.title: Schedule Automated Reports
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule HH:MM [filter=name] to generate daily reports at specific UTC times, posting performance screenshots and leaderboards automatically

implementationSteps.title: Customize and Generate Reports
implementationSteps.description: Set custom point weights with /set_points, configure colors with /set_colors, and use /recreate [filter=name] to generate immediate reports with current data

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Complete reference guide for all X Bot commands. Commands are organized by category for easy navigation. Basic Commands /help Display all available bot commands with brief descriptions. Usage: /help Parameters: None Response: Complete list of commands organized by category. Example: Copy /help X API Filtering Setup X Bot's filtering system allows you to define exactly what X (Twitter) content to track and analyze. You can track specific accounts, monitor keywords and hashtags, follow cryptocurrency cashtags, see who's mentioning important accounts, and filter out noise with exclusions. The filtering system is highly flexible and supports complex setups through named filters , which allow you to create multiple independent tracking configurations. This is particularly useful for KOL teams managing multiple clients, projects running different campaigns, or anyone who needs to separate different tracking scenarios. Each named filter can have its own set of accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and even independent report schedules. Account Management Track X (Twitter) accounts to monitor their posts and engagement metrics. /add_accounts Add one or more X accounts to track. Usage: /add_accounts @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X (Twitter) handles to track (with @ symbol) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Tracks all posts from specified accounts Monitors engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes accounts in leaderboard rankings /remove_accounts Remove one or more X accounts from tracking. Usage: /remove_accounts @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_accounts View all currently tracked X accounts. Usage: /get_accounts [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show accounts for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked X accounts, or accounts in the specified filter. Keyword Management Track specific keywords and hashtags to monitor community discussions. /add_keywords Add keywords or hashtags to track. Usage: /add_keywords "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to track (use quotes for multi-word phrases) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Hashtags should include the # symbol. /remove_keywords Remove keywords or hashtags from tracking. Usage: /remove_keywords "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_keywords View all currently tracked keywords and hashtags. Usage: /get_keywords [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show keywords for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked keywords and hashtags, or keywords in the specified filter. Cashtag Management Track cryptocurrency cashtags (e.g., $BTC, $ETH) to monitor token discussions. /add_cashtags Add cashtags to track. Usage: /add_cashtags $TAG1 $TAG2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: $TAG1 $TAG2 ... : Cashtags to track (with $ symbol) filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Cashtags must include the $ symbol. /remove_cashtags Remove cashtags from tracking. Usage: /remove_cashtags $TAG1 $TAG2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: $TAG1 $TAG2 ... : Cashtags to stop tracking filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_cashtags View all currently tracked cashtags. Usage: /get_cashtags [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show cashtags for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all tracked cashtags, or cashtags in the specified filter. Mention Management Track X account mentions to see who's talking about specific accounts. /add_mentions Add X accounts to track mentions of. Usage: /add_mentions @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to track mentions of filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Tracks posts that mention the specified accounts Shows who is talking about these accounts Includes mention frequency in reports /remove_mentions Remove X accounts from mention tracking. Usage: /remove_mentions @account1 @account2 ... [filter=name] Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop tracking mentions of filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_mentions View all accounts currently tracked for mentions. Usage: /get_mentions [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show mentions for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all accounts being tracked for mentions, or mentions in the specified filter. Exclusion Management Exclude specific keywords or hashtags from reports to filter out noise. /add_exclusions Add keywords or hashtags to exclude from tracking. Usage: /add_exclusions "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to exclude filter=name (optional): Assign to a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Excludes posts containing these keywords from reports Helps filter out spam, scams, or irrelevant content Improves report quality /remove_exclusions Remove keywords or hashtags from exclusion list. Usage: /remove_exclusions "keyword1" "keyword2" ... [filter=name] Parameters: "keyword1" "keyword2" ... : Keywords or hashtags to stop excluding filter=name (optional): Remove from a named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_exclusions View all currently excluded keywords and hashtags. Usage: /get_exclusions [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show exclusions for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: List of all excluded keywords and hashtags, or exclusions in the specified filter. Ignore List Management Ignore specific X accounts so they don't appear in leaderboards (useful for hiding bots, test accounts, or team admins). /add_ignore_list Add X accounts to the ignore list. Usage: /add_ignore_list @account1 @account2 ... Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to ignore in leaderboards Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Hides specified accounts from leaderboard rankings Posts are still tracked but not shown in public reports Useful for internal/test accounts /remove_ignore_list Remove X accounts from the ignore list. Usage: /remove_ignore_list @account1 @account2 ... Parameters: @account1 @account2 ... : X handles to stop ignoring Admin Only: Yes Examples: /get_ignore_list View all accounts currently on the ignore list. Usage: /get_ignore_list Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: List of all ignored X accounts. Named Filters Named filters allow you to create multiple tracking configurations for different purposes (e.g., different clients, campaigns, or projects). Understanding Named Filters Named filters let you: Track different sets of accounts/keywords for different purposes Generate separate reports for different clients or campaigns Keep configurations organized and separate How to use named filters: 1 Create a named filter Add items with the filter=name parameter, for example: 2 Generate reports for a named filter Use the recreate command with the filter parameter: 3 View filter configuration Inspect items in a filter: Examples of Named Filter Usage Example 1: Multiple Client Tracking (KOL Team) Example 2: Different Campaign Tracking (Project) Schedule Management Automate report generation with scheduled daily reports. /set_schedule Schedule automated daily reports. Usage: /set_schedule HH:MM [filter=name] Parameters: HH:MM : Time in 24-hour format (UTC timezone) filter=name (optional): Schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Generates automated daily reports at the specified time Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to the group Time is in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) Timezone Conversion Examples: New York (EST): 12:00 UTC = 07:00 EST London (GMT): 12:00 UTC = 12:00 GMT Singapore (SGT): 12:00 UTC = 20:00 SGT /get_schedule View current report schedule. Usage: /get_schedule [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Show schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: No Examples: Response: Current scheduled report time in UTC, or message if no schedule is set. /delete_schedule Cancel automated daily reports. Usage: /delete_schedule [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Delete schedule for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Removes scheduled report generation Reports can still be generated manually with /recreate Report Generation Generate analytics reports manually. /recreate Generate a new analytics report immediately. Usage: /recreate [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Generate report for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes (for manual generation) Examples: What this does: Creates performance report with current data Generates leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Posts performance screenshot to the group Shows engagement metrics and rankings Note: Report generation can take 1-2 minutes depending on data volume. /report Alternative command to generate analytics report. Usage: /report [filter=name] Parameters: filter=name (optional): Generate report for a specific named filter Admin Only: Yes Examples: Note: Functions identically to /recreate . Customization Customize report appearance and scoring to match your brand. /set_points Customize engagement point weights for performance scoring. Usage: /set_points <likes> <retweets> <replies> <quotes> <bookmarks> <impressions> Parameters: <likes> : Point weight for likes (default: 1) <retweets> : Point weight for retweets (default: 2) <replies> : Point weight for replies (default: 1.5) <quotes> : Point weight for quote tweets (default: 3) <bookmarks> : Point weight for bookmarks (default: 2) <impressions> : Point weight for impressions (default: 0.001) Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Customizes how performance scores are calculated Higher weights give more importance to that engagement type Affects leaderboard rankings Default Formula: /get_points View current engagement point weights. Usage: /get_points Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current point weights for all engagement types. /set_colors Customize colors used in performance reports. Usage: /set_colors <likes> <retweets> <replies> <quotes> <bookmarks> <impressions> Parameters: <likes> : Hex color code for likes (default: #FF6B6B) <retweets> : Hex color code for retweets (default: #4ECDC4) <replies> : Hex color code for replies (default: #45B7D1) <quotes> : Hex color code for quotes (default: #FFA07A) <bookmarks> : Hex color code for bookmarks (default: #98D8C8) <impressions> : Hex color code for impressions (default: #F7DC6F) Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Customizes visual appearance of reports Applies to performance screenshots Helps match your brand colors Note: Colors must be valid hex codes (e.g., #FF6B6B). /get_colors View current color configuration. Usage: /get_colors Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current hex color codes for all engagement types. /set_top_title Customize the title for the main leaderboard section. Usage: /set_top_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for main leaderboard section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Replaces default "Top Accounts by Performance" title Adds custom branding to reports Title appears in performance screenshots /set_best_title Customize the title for the best tweet section. Usage: /set_best_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for best tweet section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: /set_engagement_title Customize the title for the engagement scoring section. Usage: /set_engagement_title "Custom Title" "#HexColor" Parameters: "Custom Title" : Title text for engagement scoring section "#HexColor" : Hex color code for the title Admin Only: Yes Examples: Project Settings Configure project metadata to enhance your presence on xbot.ninja. /set_project_name Set your project or team name. Usage: /set_project_name "Project Name" Parameters: "Project Name" : Name of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets project name displayed on xbot.ninja Appears in public leaderboards if you meet listing criteria Helps projects and KOLs get discovered /get_project_name View current project name. Usage: /get_project_name Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_description Set your project or team description. Usage: /set_project_description "Description text" Parameters: "Description text" : Brief description of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets description shown on xbot.ninja Helps users understand what your project/team does Appears in featured projects list /get_project_description View current project description. Usage: /get_project_description Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_logo Set your project or team logo URL. Usage: /set_project_logo "https://url-to-logo.png" Parameters: "https://url-to-logo.png" : Direct URL to your logo image Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets logo displayed on xbot.ninja Provides visual branding for your project/team Logo appears in featured projects showcase Note: Logo should be square format (1:1 ratio) and accessible via HTTPS. /get_project_logo View current project logo URL. Usage: /get_project_logo Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_long_description Set detailed project or team description. Usage: /set_project_long_description "Long description text" Parameters: "Long description text" : Detailed description of your project or team Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets extended description for project detail pages Provides more context about your project/team Helps users make informed decisions /get_project_long_description View current long project description. Usage: /get_project_long_description Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: /set_project_urls Set project or team URLs (website, social media, etc.). Usage: /set_project_urls "url1" "url2" "url3" ... Parameters: "url1" "url2" ... : URLs for website, social media, documentation, etc. Admin Only: Yes Examples: What this does: Sets links displayed on xbot.ninja Provides ways for users to learn more about your project/team Helps drive traffic to your platforms /get_project_urls View current project URLs. Usage: /get_project_urls Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Getting Credits X Bot includes 100 FREE credits to get you started. Each credit corresponds to one post tracked in your filtering setup (accounts, keywords, cashtags, and mentions you've configured). Once you've used your free credits, you can purchase additional credits to continue tracking. /buy Purchase X Bot Pro Edition credits using BWS tokens. Usage: /buy Parameters: None Where: Works in both group chats and Direct Messages (DM) with the bot 🔒 PRIVACY RECOMMENDED: Use Direct Message (DM) for Private Purchases For complete privacy, send /buy in a Direct Message to @BWS_X_Bot instead of in the group chat. This keeps the entire purchase conversation private - only you will see the purchase link and transaction details. Examples: Recommended: In Direct Message with bot (completely private) In group chat (visible to everyone in the group) What this does: Provides secure purchase link for BWS token credit purchase Redirects to purchase page at xbot.ninja Credits are added to your group immediately after successful purchase How to purchase privately in DM: 1 Open a Direct Message with the bot Open a Direct Message with @BWS_X_Bot . 2 Send the buy command Send /buy command in the DM (not in the group). 3 Receive private link Bot sends you a private purchase link that only you can see. 4 Complete purchase Complete your purchase privately - no one in the group will see any part of the transaction. 5 Credits applied Credits are automatically added to your group upon successful payment. /subscription View your current Pro subscription status. Usage: /subscription Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Current subscription status, expiration date, and available credits. /get_credits View available Pro credits for your group. Usage: /get_credits Parameters: None Admin Only: No Example: Response: Number of remaining Pro credits for report generation and other Pro features. Command Tips & Best Practices General Tips Use Quotes for Multi-Word Terms: Always use quotes when adding keywords with spaces: Include Symbols: Don't forget @ for accounts, # for hashtags, $ for cashtags: Named Filters for Organization: Use named filters to keep different tracking scenarios organized: Schedule in UTC: Remember that schedule times are in UTC timezone. Convert from your local time. Performance Optimization Start Small: Begin with a few accounts/keywords and expand based on results Use Exclusions: Filter out spam and irrelevant content with exclusions Regular Reports: Set daily schedules to track trends over time Named Filters: Separate different campaigns or clients for clearer insights Report Quality Balanced Tracking: Track both accounts and keywords for comprehensive insights Clean Data: Use ignore list to hide test accounts and bots Custom Scoring: Adjust point weights to reflect what matters most to your community Branding: Set custom titles and colors to match your brand Privacy and Security DM for Privacy: Use Direct Messages for sensitive commands like /buy Admin Commands: Only admins can configure tracking and settings Public Reports: Anyone in the group can view reports and current configuration Filter Isolation: Named filters keep different client/campaign data separate Troubleshooting 1 No Data in Reports Verify accounts/keywords are configured: /get_accounts , /get_keywords Wait a few hours for X API to collect data Ensure tracked accounts are public 2 Reports Not Generating Check schedule is set: /get_schedule Verify time is in UTC Manually test with /recreate 3 Bot Not Responding Verify bot has admin status in the group Check if bot was removed or banned Try removing and re-adding the bot Advanced Usage Multi-Client KOL Team Setup: Comprehensive Project Tracking: This command reference is for X Bot Beta Version. Commands and features are subject to change as the platform improves. Previous X Bot Installation Next Getting Support Last updated 8 days ago Was this helpful? pages.title: X Bot Installation | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Step-by-step installation guide for X Bot covering three setup paths: Crypto Project tracking, Individual KOL monitoring, and KOL Team performance management. Includes prerequisites, configuration instructions, verification steps, and troubleshooting for common issues. Provides specific examples for each use case with command sequences and customization options.

codeExamples.description: Basic setup for crypto projects to track official account and project cashtag
Schedule automated daily reports at 12:00 UTC

useCases: Set up X analytics for crypto project community tracking
Create personal KOL portfolio with verifiable performance metrics
Configure team analytics dashboard for KOL agencies
Track client project campaigns with separate named filters

implementationSteps.title: Add Bot to Telegram Group
implementationSteps.description: Search for @BWS_X_Bot, add to your Telegram group (project, personal, or team), and make it an admin with required permissions

implementationSteps.title: Configure Tracking
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts for X handles, /add_cashtags for tokens, /add_keywords for topics. For teams, add all member accounts and client project cashtags

implementationSteps.title: Set Schedule and Generate Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule HH:MM for automated daily reports at your preferred time (UTC), then run /recreate to verify tracking is working correctly

implementationSteps.title: Optional: Add Metadata and Customize
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_name, /set_kol_name, or /set_team_name commands to get featured on xbot.ninja. Customize with /set_points, /set_colors, and /set_top_title for branded reports

The setup process differs based on your use case — choose the path that matches your needs. Prerequisites Before installing X Bot, ensure you have: A Telegram account Admin access to a Telegram group (or ability to create one) An X (Twitter) account you want to track Basic familiarity with Telegram bots and group management Choose Your Installation Path Select the setup guide that matches your use case: Crypto Project Setup : Track your project's X engagement and community activity Individual KOL Setup : Monitor your personal X performance metrics KOL Team Setup : Track multiple team members' performance in one place Crypto Project Setup Goal: Track your project's X engagement and community activity (optionally use for competitions, prizes, or airdrops) 1 Find and Add the Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your project's main Telegram group Make the bot an admin with the following settings: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 2 Create Your X Analytics Query The following is a simple example for first-time installation. You can customize tracking later. Track your official X account: Track your project's cashtag: Example: What this does: Tracks all posts from your project's X account Monitors posts mentioning your cashtag (e.g., $SOL ) Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings 3 Schedule Daily Reports Set up automatic daily analytics reports: What this does: Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to your group Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Keeps community engaged with regular updates Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement. 4 Verify Installation Force an initial report to verify everything is working: What this does: Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking Shows leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Confirms tracking is working correctly Gives you a preview of daily automated reports 5 Add Project Metadata (Optional) Add project information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery Helps investors find projects with authentic community traction Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly 6 Customize Your Reports (Optional) Personalize reports with custom branding. Set Custom Point Weights (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Bookmarks, Impressions): Set Custom Colors: Set Custom Titles: Individual KOL Setup Goal: Get publicly listed on xbot.ninja to showcase your competencies and performance metrics. 1 Create Your Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it something like "My X Analytics" or "KOL Performance Tracker" This can be a private group for personal tracking Tip: Keep this group private or share it with potential clients to showcase your metrics. 2 Find and Add X Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your newly created group Make the bot an admin with: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Track Your X Account Monitor your personal X account performance: Example: What this does: Tracks all your posts and engagement metrics Builds a verifiable portfolio of your performance Enables discovery on xbot.ninja Provides data for client reports 4 Add Your Topics Track keywords relevant to your content: What this does: Shows which topics you're covering Demonstrates your niche expertise Tracks community engagement with your topics Helps projects find you based on relevant keywords 5 Enable Regular Reports Get daily performance insights: What this does: Sends daily performance reports at 09:00 UTC Tracks engagement trends over time Helps identify top-performing content Provides data for client reporting 6 Create Your First Report Generate your first analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate snapshot of your performance Shows engagement metrics and rankings Verifies tracking is working correctly Gives you shareable proof of your influence 7 Add Your Profile Metadata (Optional) Add profile information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your KOL profile on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire you Showcases engagement metrics and expertise publicly To get publicly listed: Maintain a minimum performance threshold by posting and engaging consistently Build authentic engagement (avoid vanity metrics) Monitor daily reports to track progress toward listing Note: Featured KOLs are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. KOL Team Setup Goal: Track team performance accurately and show clients real, measurable results 1 Create Team Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it "KOL Team Analytics" or "[Team Name] Performance Tracker" This will be your shared analytics dashboard Tip: Make this group private and invite only team members and trusted clients. 2 Find and Add Bot as Admin Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your team group Make the bot an admin: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Add Team Members Invite all KOL team members to the group: Click the group name to open group info Tap "Add Members" Select all team members Ensure everyone can see the analytics and reports 4 Track All Team Accounts Add all team members' X accounts for tracking: Example: What this does: Tracks team members' posts and engagement Creates team-wide leaderboards Enables performance comparisons Provides aggregate metrics for client reports 5 Set Projects You Work For Monitor client projects with cashtags: Example: What this does: Tracks team engagement with client tokens Shows which members promote which projects Demonstrates ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Identifies top performers per campaign 6 Schedule Team Reports Set up daily team performance reports: What this does: Generates daily reports at 10:00 UTC Posts leaderboards showing top team members Tracks trends for each project Provides regular updates for team and clients Tip: Schedule for team standups or planning meetings. 7 Create Your First Report Generate your first team analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate team performance snapshot Shows individual and aggregate metrics Verifies all accounts are tracked correctly Provides shareable data for client presentations 8 Add Team Metadata (Optional) Feature your team on xbot.ninja : What this does: Displays your KOL team on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire your team Showcases team engagement metrics publicly 9 Customize Team Branding (Optional) Personalize reports with custom titles: 10 Exclude Internal Accounts (Optional) Hide internal/test accounts from leaderboards: Verification and Testing 1 Check bot status The bot should appear as an admin in your group 2 Test a command Run /help to see all available commands 3 Generate a report Run /recreate to create your first analytics report 4 Review the output Ensure accounts and keywords are tracked correctly 5 Check scheduled reports Wait for your scheduled time to verify automation Common Issues and Solutions Bot Not Responding Possible causes: Bot not made admin in the group Bot was removed or banned Network connectivity issues Solutions: Verify bot has admin status Remove and re-add the bot Check Telegram server status No Data in Reports Possible causes: Accounts or keywords not configured Tracking period too short (wait a few hours) X accounts have privacy restrictions Solutions: Run /get_accounts and /get_keywords to verify configuration Wait for X API to collect data (can take a few hours) Ensure tracked X accounts are public Reports Not Generating Possible causes: No schedule set Schedule time in wrong timezone (should be UTC) Insufficient data for report generation Solutions: Run /get_schedule to verify configuration Set schedule with /set_schedule HH:MM (UTC time) Manually generate report with /recreate to test Installation takes just a few minutes. Once configured, X Bot runs automatically, providing scheduled analytics to help you track, measure, and improve your X engagement. pages.title: X Bot Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of X Bot as a trusted X (Twitter) analytics platform for crypto communities. Explains the accuracy advantage of using official X API versus third-party tools, addresses problems with measuring authentic engagement, and describes dual-purpose platform serving project owners, KOLs, and investors. Includes performance scoring formula, curation process, and benefits of transparent metrics.

Identify legitimate projects vs artificial engagement
Hire influencers based on proven performance data

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Overview What is X Bot? X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. Key Features Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post Example Calculation: For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions Customization & Branding Custom point weights for different engagement types Branded colors for visual reports Custom titles for leaderboard sections Project metadata (name, logo, description, URLs) Named filters for different tracking scenarios Automated Reporting Schedule daily reports at specific times (UTC) Automatic performance screenshots Leaderboards showing top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Historical tracking and trend analysis Dual-Purpose Analytics Platform X Bot serves multiple distinct but complementary audiences: For Project Owners Get a trusted measure of community engagement and hired KOLs' real profitability Measure your community's authentic X engagement and impact Track the real profitability and ROI of hired KOL campaigns Discover KOL teams with proven performance metrics Hire influencers who deliver actual engagement and results Monitor KOL performance with transparent, verifiable data Avoid influencers with fake followers or inflated metrics What you get: Trusted measurement of community engagement levels Real performance data on KOL teams and their ROI Comparative metrics across multiple influencers Evidence of authentic community building Data-driven decisions for influencer partnerships For KOLs and KOL Teams Showcase your competencies and get discovered Build a public portfolio of verifiable performance metrics Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Track team performance accurately to show clients measurable results Stand out from competitors with transparent data Demonstrate real ROI to potential clients For Investors Discover crypto projects with authentic community traction Visit xbot.ninja to explore our new investment discovery feature: Find projects with genuine X activity and real engagement Identify legitimate investment opportunities backed by actual community activity Analyze projects based on verifiable metrics, not vanity numbers Make data-driven investment decisions What to look for: Consistent engagement across multiple metrics Growing community participation Authentic conversations and discussions Real influencer involvement How Projects and KOLs Get Featured X Bot curates high-quality data from projects and KOL teams through a selection process. 1 X Bot Installation Must have successfully installed and configured X Bot in their Telegram community. Demonstrates commitment to transparent analytics. 2 Minimum Performance Threshold Achieve a minimum performance score indicating sustained X activity, showing genuine engagement over time. 3 Active Community Engagement Consistent posting and meaningful interactions, authentic community growth patterns, and regular engagement from community members. 4 Quality Content Standards High-quality X presence with genuine engagement metrics, no artificial inflation or bot-driven activity, and authentic interactions and conversations. Why Curation Matters The curation process ensures quality for all stakeholders: Investors: Find legitimate projects with real community backing Project Owners: Discover KOL teams that deliver actual engagement and ROI KOLs: Get discovered by serious projects looking for proven performers Everyone: Access transparent, verifiable metrics reflecting authentic activity All projects and KOL teams are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. Use Cases Crypto Projects Boost community X engagement with competitions and prizes Track your project's official X account Monitor community hashtags and cashtags Run engagement competitions with transparent leaderboards Reward top performers with prizes Build authentic community participation Individual KOLs Get publicly listed to showcase your competencies Create a personal analytics tracking group Monitor your X account performance Build a verifiable portfolio of engagement metrics Get discovered by projects seeking influencers Prove your value with transparent data KOL Teams Track team performance and show clients real results Set up shared analytics for your entire team Track all team members' X accounts in one place Monitor project hashtags you're promoting Generate team performance reports Demonstrate ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Benefits Summary Transparency All metrics are verifiable and sourced directly from X (Twitter) No manipulation or artificial inflation Real-time data updates Authenticity Performance scoring weighs meaningful engagement over vanity metrics Curated selection ensures quality participants Continuous monitoring maintains standards Actionable Insights Daily automated reports with actionable data Leaderboards identifying top performers Historical trends and performance tracking Customizable views for different analysis needs Community Building Gamification through leaderboards and competitions Recognition for top contributors Customizable views for different analysis needs Previous X Bot Next X Bot Installation Last updated 9 days ago Was this helpful? Copy Performance Score = (Likes × 1) + (Retweets × 2) + (Replies × 1.5) + (Quotes × 3) + (Bookmarks × 2) + (Impressions × 0.001) Copy Performance Score = (50 × 1) + (10 × 2) + (8 × 1.5) + (3 × 3) + (15 × 2) + (5,000 × 0.001) = 50 + 20 + 12 + 9 + 30 + 5 = 126 points

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains BWS platform fee structure and blockchain transaction costs. Covers how BWS manages network fees, token pool usage, real-time USD to network token conversion, and $BWS token requirements. Includes pricing calculator and billing examples with 0.01 USD minimum billable unit.

codeExamples.description: Example pricing calculation for creating 100 NFTs using BWS.NFT.zK and BWS.IPFS.Upload on Polygon blockchain

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees Fees Calculator API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start Next API How Tos Last updated 8 days ago Was this helpful? Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? Was this helpful? pages.title: Quick Start | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Quick Start guide demonstrating how to write 'Hello World!' to blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash in one API call. Covers API key authentication, making first blockchain request to Matchain network, and checking job status asynchronously with complete code examples in curl, JavaScript, and Python.

codeExamples.description: Write 'Hello World!' message to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash API
Check blockchain job status and retrieve transaction receipt using jobId from previous call
JavaScript example for writing data to blockchain using jQuery AJAX

useCases: Write immutable messages to Matchain blockchain network
Save key-value data pairs to blockchain without managing wallets
Retrieve blockchain transaction receipts and Certificate of Trust
Integrate blockchain storage into Web2 applications with simple API
Test blockchain interactions without handling network fees

implementationSteps.title: Get API Key
implementationSteps.description: Obtain your API key for authentication. All API requests must include this key or they will return an error.

implementationSteps.title: Make Blockchain Write Request
implementationSteps.description: Call the /v1/smart-contracts/call endpoint with BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to write your message to Matchain blockchain. Receive a jobId in the response.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the jobId to call /v1/smart-contracts/fetch endpoint to check completion status. Blockchain operations are asynchronous and may take time to complete.

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Results
implementationSteps.description: Once completed, fetch returns the blockchain receipt, transaction hash, transaction URL, and Certificate of Trust for verification.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). Learn how to Get your API Key . Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save a message to the blockchain. Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.Blockchain.Hash uses blockchain as an efficient hash database, allowing businesses to store and retrieve data using unique keys. The solution provides blockchain transparency, immutable records, and includes a Certificate of Trust for each entry to ensure data integrity and authenticity.
useCases: Manage client data with unique customer IDs as hash keys
Store transaction records for auditing and verification
Enable quick data retrieval for reporting purposes
Ensure data integrity through blockchain immutability
Provide clients with verifiable Certificate of Trust

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. Available Networks We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Operations Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API).
We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files.

When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network.
Get the list of your IPFS files
Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file:
the IPFS Content Identifier (CID),
and the URIs to easily access the file
web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS IPFS service leverages InterPlanetary File System for decentralized, permanent storage with content addressing. It supports images, PDF, and JSON files with BWS IPFS Gateway (ipfs.bws.ninja) providing HTTP access. Key features include decentralized storage, content immutability, and global accessibility.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and assets for blockchain applications
Host decentralized content for web projects
Share structured JSON data with transparency
Store tamper-proof files with content addressing
Enable global accessibility through IPFS network
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. Previous BWS.IPFS.Upload Next Operations Last updated 8 days ago Was this helpful? BWS IPFS Gateway Key Features of IPFS Supported File Types Was this helpful?

pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains NFT attributes (traits) which are specific characteristics that define each NFT's uniqueness. Details how traits are structured using trait_type and value pairs within the metadata attributes array, and how BWS.NFT.zK allows creation of NFTs with custom traits.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure showing how to define traits for a digital art collection of animated cats with specific attributes like fur color, eye type, and accessories

useCases: Create unique digital art collections with specific characteristics
Define rarity traits for NFT collectibles
Add custom attributes to gaming NFTs like character features
Specify metadata traits for limited edition digital assets
Build NFT collections with distinguishing properties
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive API reference for BWS.NFT.zK operations including creating NFTs, listing NFTs, transferring NFT ownership, and sending NFTs via email. Documents request/response formats, method parameters, and provides examples for each operation with detailed response structures including transaction hashes and IPFS references.

codeExamples.description: API call to create a new NFT on the Mumbai test network with name, description, image URL, and rarity attributes
Response from NFT creation API call returning a job ID for tracking the asynchronous blockchain transaction status
Completed NFT creation response showing transaction hash, IPFS hashes for metadata and image, URLs for accessing the NFT, and transfer codes for ownership management

useCases: Mint new NFTs on blockchain networks with custom metadata
List and retrieve all NFTs created within a specific time range
Transfer NFT ownership to a blockchain wallet address
Send NFTs to users via email without requiring a Web3 wallet
Track NFT creation status using asynchronous job IDs

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Call the API with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', network selection, and parameters including name, description, image URL, and attributes. Receive a jobId for tracking.

implementationSteps.title: Check Transaction Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the returned jobId to check blockchain transaction status and retrieve the completed NFT details including transaction hash and IPFS references.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer or Send NFT
implementationSteps.description: Use 'transfer' operation with wallet address or 'send' operation with email address to transfer NFT ownership, providing the nftId and destination address/email.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Operations Create NFT List NFTs Transfer NFT Send NFT by Email NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Operations BWS.NFT.zK API Operations. Create NFT This operation is asynchronous (the blockchain network takes a while to confirm transactions). Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. Create NFT Request Example cURL Create NFT Fetch Response Create NFT Fetch Response Example The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) NFT Urls Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL Transfer Codes Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. List NFTs Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. List NFT Method Parameters If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. List IPFS Files call Response LIST NFT Request Example cURL List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. List NFT Fetch Response Example Transfer NFT This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Transfer NFT Request Example cURL Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example Send NFT by Email Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) Send NFT Request Example cURL Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of NFT.zK solution covering NFT ownership models, metadata and image location on IPFS, and available blockchain networks. Explains two transfer methods: direct wallet transfers and email-based transfers with custody management, plus details on smart contract deployments across Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum networks.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT creation request using a URL for the image, which will be automatically uploaded to IPFS by the BWS.NFT.zK service
Example NFT metadata showing IPFS URI format for the image that will be stored in the NFT's metadata on IPFS

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to users with blockchain wallet addresses
Send NFTs to recipients via email without requiring Web3 wallets
Store NFT images and metadata permanently on IPFS
Deploy NFTs on multiple blockchain networks including Matchain and Polygon
Manage NFT custody before transferring to final owners

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with BWS Account
implementationSteps.description: Create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK which initially links the NFT to your BWS account for custody before transfer.

implementationSteps.title: Choose Transfer Method
implementationSteps.description: Select between transferring ownership directly to a blockchain wallet address or sending to an email address for users without Web3 wallets.

implementationSteps.title: Execute Transfer
implementationSteps.description: Use the Transfer NFT operation for wallet transfers or Send NFT operation for email transfers, with the recipient receiving notification and transfer codes for ownership claim.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview NFT Ownership NFT Data Location Available Networks Operations NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Easily create NFTs. NFT Ownership When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options: Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address. Transfer ownership to an email address. Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account. Transfer NFT Ownership to a Wallet If you want to transfer an NFT ownership to another wallet address, you can simply use the Transfer NFT operation indicating the new blockchain wallet address owner. Transfer NFT Ownership to an Email Address If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address. Email received for the new NFT owner to hold and get ownership We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to. Transfer ownership page for wasy NFT transfers Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address. NFT Metadata and Image Location NFT Image Your NFT image is always hosted in IPFS. To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as: a web addressable URL, or, as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki). API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image. In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required. NFT Metadata Your NFT metadata is always hosted in IPFS. When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS, NFT Metadata Example Your NFT blockchain transaction then points to IPFS, as you can see on the following example: NFT Creation Transaction Parameters Networks We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. We currently offer NFT creation in Polygon and Ethereum blockchains. Blockchain Network Id Description Matchain matchain Matchain blockchain. Polygon polygon Polygon blockchain. mumbai Polygon test network. Ethereum ethereum Ethereum blockchain We recommend using the Polygon Mumbai network for tests. Smart Contracts We're updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon and Ethereum networks. Please use Matchain. Click on Contract Address to check the verified contract. paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
